Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additively Manufacturable High-Strength Aluminum Alloys with Coarsening-Resistant Microstructures Achieved via Rapid Solidification

Composition_Info.Note: Equivalent to Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%; Composition designed using CALPHAD-based ICME combined with Bayesian optimization

Process_Text.original: Powder manufactured by ultrasonic atomization; specimens fabricated by LPBF AM (laser powder bed fusion) with optimized power and scan speed parameters to achieve highest density; samples verified to be crack-free
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF AM of ultrasonic atomized powder with optimized processing parameters, crack-free specimens

Process_Info.Equipment: LPBF system (DMG MORI LT30 mentioned in acknowledgements)

Microstructure_Text.original: Non-textured equiaxed grains from EBSD; melt pool boundaries and grains visible at low magnification; primary Al3Ni phase uniformly distributed; L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) identified via TEM with {001}-type superlattice reflections; ordered nanoclusters within matrix with L12-type ordering; Zr-rich precipitates at grain boundaries; core-shell structure for Er and Zr distribution in L12 phases
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed grains; L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) with coherent interfaces; Al3Ni primary phases; metastable ternary Al23Ni6M4 phases at grain boundaries

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM-EDS: JEOL JSM7900F FE-SEM with Oxford Ultim Max 100 mm2 detector; Al3Ni and Zr-rich phases identified; ternary phases (Er+Ni) at grain boundaries
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.STEM-HAADF: Thermo Fisher Themis Z 60-300 kV at 300 kV; L12 nanoprecipitates with superlattice reflections; atomic resolution EDS maps showing Al/Er/Zr segregation
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.APT: Cameca LEAP 4000 HR; voltage-pulsing mode at 40 K; Zr-rich L12 precipitates confirmed; ternary Al-Ni-Er/Zr phase composition verified
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EBSD: ThermoFisher Apreo HiVac SEM with EDAX Hikari EBSD detector at 15 kV, step size 0.045 um; equiaxed grains, non-textured
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.CT-Scan: Zeiss Xradia Crystal CT; no cracks or pores detected in scanned volumes

Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8 type specimens from 6x6x6 mm3 cubes

Properties_Info.Note: Peak strength reached after aging; as-built strength lower
Ductility with 25% sacrifice in strength from peak aged condition

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; parallel to build direction and on top of samples
Properties_Info.Note: As-built condition; comparable to wrought Al 7075

Composition_Info.Note: Equivalent to Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%; Peak aged condition at 400 C for 8 hours

Process_Text.original: LPBF AM specimens (28x6x6 and 6x6x6 mm3) subsequently aged at 400 C for 8 hours (isothermal aging); power and scan speed optimized for highest density
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF AM followed by aging at 400 C for 8 hours

Microstructure_Text.original: L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) with {001}-type superlattice reflections in FFT; metastable ternary Al23Ni6M4 phases transformed to L12 during aging; ordered nanoclusters within matrix with average area 1.90 nm2 and radius 0.70 nm; Al3Ni primary phases at grain boundaries; Zr-rich precipitates at grain boundaries with core-shell Er/Zr distribution; 79% of strength at service temperature governed by nano-meter scale L12 precipitates
Microstructure_Text.simplified: L12 nanoprecipitates (1-5 nm) formed from transformation of metastable ternary phases during aging; coarsening-resistant microstructure

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.STEM-HAADF: L12 nanoprecipitates with {001}-type superlattice reflections; atomic resolution showing ordered bright/dim {110} planes; core-shell structure for Er and Zr distribution
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.APT: Zr-rich precipitates segmented at 3.0% Zr isosurface; L12 Al3(Er,Zr) precipitation confirmed; ternary phase composition near Al23Ni6M4 predicted

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; aged 8h at 400 C
Properties_Info.Note: Peak tensile strength; 50% higher than Gong et al.'s benchmark printable Al alloy (275 MPa)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; aged 8h at 400 C
at room temperature; peak hardness after 8-10h aging at 400 C
Properties_Info.Note: Peak hardness; 50% higher than AM benchmark alloy (133 HV); higher than wrought Al 7075 under identical conditions

Composition_Info.Note: Equivalent to Al-2.33Er-3.19Zr-2.73Ni wt.%; Aged 48 hours at 400 C showing strength stability

Process_Text.original: LPBF AM specimens subsequently aged at 400 C for 48 hours (isothermal aging); same optimized LPBF parameters as 8h aged samples
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF AM followed by aging at 400 C for 48 hours

Microstructure_Text.original: Coarsening-resistant L12 phases maintained at nanometer scale after extended aging; strength maintained without significant decrease after 48h aging at 400 C
Microstructure_Text.simplified: L12 phases maintain nanometer scale during extended aging showing coarsening resistance

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; aged 48h at 400 C
at room temperature; after 48h aging at 400 C
Properties_Info.Note: Hardness maintained without significant decrease from 8h peak condition

Composition_Info.Note: Identical molar percentage of Er and Zr as ML-optimized alloy but without Ni; L12 phases form directly during rapid solidification

Process_Text.original: Control sample fabricated by induction melting and laser scanning to mimic AM conditions; processed identically to ML-optimized alloy except without Ni
Process_Text.simplified: Induction melted and laser scanned control sample without Ni addition

Process_Info.Equipment: Induction melting furnace with laser scanning

Microstructure_Text.original: L12 phases form directly during rapid solidification (no metastable ternary phase); SEM-EDS shows several large clusters of micrometer-scale Zr-rich phases within grain interiors; these clusters are not present in the Ni-containing designed composition
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Direct L12 formation during rapid solidification; micrometer-scale Zr-rich clusters inside grains

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM-EDS: Large clusters of micrometer-scale Zr-rich areas inside grains identified

Properties_Info.Note: 34% lower than ML-optimized composition with ternary phases; W/O ternary control in Figure 1

Composition_Info.Note: Same composition as ML-optimized but processed by conventional induction melting (slow solidification) for comparison

Process_Text.original: Induction melting (casting) of the same alloy composition; slower solidification compared to AM and laser scanning; SEM-EDS shows large micrometer-size ternary phases (Figure S15, Supporting Information)
Process_Text.simplified: Conventional induction melting (casting) - slow solidification for comparison with rapid solidification AM route

Microstructure_Text.original: Large micrometer-size ternary phases (Al23Ni6M4) and Al3M phases formed during slow solidification (Figure S15, Supporting Information); slower solidification via induction melting leads to microscale ternary and Al3M phases
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Microscale ternary and Al3M phases due to slow solidification

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM-EDS: Large micrometer-size ternary phases identified; Figure S15

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; after aging 8h at 400 C
Properties_Info.Note: 80% lower than aged AM sample (200 HV); demonstrates concept that rapid solidification enables submicron-scale ternary phases for strength enhancement

Composition_Info.Note: Benchmark printable Al alloy from Gong et al. (PCT/US2018/062761); designed without ML techniques; represents prior art in same compositional space

Process_Text.original: Same processing methodology as ML-optimized alloy for valid comparison; induction melted and laser scanned or AM fabricated
Process_Text.simplified: Same processing route as ML-optimized alloy (induction melting/laser scanning or AM)

Microstructure_Text.original: Benchmark alloy with L12 and ternary phases; designed within same compositional space and target properties as ML-optimized alloy but without ML techniques; has lower L12 content and higher coarsening metric than ML-optimized design
Microstructure_Text.simplified: L12 strengthened microstructure with ternary phases; lower L12 content and higher coarsening rate than ML-optimized

Properties_Info.Note: Gong et al.'s benchmark printable Al alloy; ML-optimized alloy has 50% higher yield strength (395 MPa vs 275 MPa)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; after aging 8h at 400 C; laser-scanned condition
Properties_Info.Note: ML-optimized alloy has 50% higher hardness (147 HV vs 100 HV after laser scanning; 200 HV vs 133 HV after AM)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; after aging 8h at 400 C; AM condition
Properties_Info.Note: AM benchmark; ML-optimized has 50% higher hardness (200 HV vs 133 HV)

Composition_Info.Note: Matrix of 9 compositions surrounding predicted optimum with varying Er, Zr, Y, Yb, Ni concentrations (at.% ranges: Er=[0-0.4], Zr=[0-2], Y=[0-1], Yb=[0-1], Ni=[0-4]); samples fabricated by induction melting and laser scanning for rapid validation; Ni=1.33 and Zr=1 showed highest hardness increase with aging

Process_Text.original: Rapid experimental validation workflow; small-scale samples fabricated by induction melting, then laser scanning to mimic heating and solidification conditions of AM; multiple path laser scanning mimicking printing one layer
Process_Text.simplified: Induction melting followed by laser scanning to rapidly validate composition predictions; 9 compositions tested

Process_Info.Equipment: Induction melting furnace with laser scanning

Microstructure_Text.original: Prediction validated with no cracks observed after solidification for all 9 samples; CT-scan showed no cracks or pores in highest HCS samples; Al 7075 comparison showed clear hot cracking
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Crack-free solidification for all 9 compositions; validates printability predictions

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.CT-Scan: No cracks or pores detected in highest HCS composition samples (Al-0.4Er-1Zr-1.33Ni and Al-0.4Er-2Zr-0Ni); Al 7075 showed 1.43% defect density

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; aged 8h at 400 C; best performing composition (Ni=1.33, Zr=1 at.%)
Properties_Info.Note: 50% higher than benchmark alloy (100 HV) after laser scanning validation; highest increase in hardness with aging among all 9 compositions
